exercises.prompt: In a past narrative, which tense paints the background scenery?
exercises.explanation: Imparfait = background; passé composé = events; plus-que-parfait = before; conditionnel = future-in-the-past.

exercises.prompt: Future seen from the past: « Ce café ___ plus tard mon lieu préféré. »
exercises.explanation: In a past narrative, the future is expressed by the conditional: deviendrait.

exercises.template: Il est conseillé {{b1}} se renseigner avant de partir.

exercises.explanation: Negative imperative for recommendations: Ne manquez pas… !
Éviter DE + infinitive: évitez de nager ici.

exercises.prompt: What had happened BEFORE — complete the auxiliary (one word).
exercises.template: Les peintres {{b1}} immortalisé la ville bien avant mon arrivée.
exercises.explanation: Anteriority in a narrative → plus-que-parfait: avaient immortalisé.

exercises.prompt: Match each travel word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "It's advisable to check beforehand."
Listen to the guide. What's the recommendation, and what's the warning?
exercises.transcript: Ne manquez pas le marché, mais évitez d'y aller à midi : il y a trop de monde.

options.text: Don't miss the market — but avoid going at noon, it's too crowded
Avoid the market — it's only open at noon
Go to the market at noon, when it's busiest

exercises.explanation: Recommendation: ne manquez pas le marché. Warning: évitez d'y aller à midi.

exercises.prompt: Read this page from a travel diary and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Ce matin-là, je flânais dans le souk quand un marchand m'a appelé. Il avait préparé du thé à la menthe pour ses visiteurs. Nous avons parlé pendant une heure. Ce thé, je ne le savais pas encore, deviendrait mon meilleur souvenir du voyage.
exercises.passageEn: That morning, I was strolling in the souk when a merchant called out to me. He had prepared mint tea for his visitors. We talked for an hour. That tea — I didn't know it yet — would become my best memory of the trip.

questions.prompt: What had the merchant done BEFORE calling out (plus-que-parfait)?
« Ce thé deviendrait mon meilleur souvenir » — why the conditional?

options.text: It's the future seen from a past moment

exercises.prompt: « Je flânais dans le souk quand un marchand m'a appelé. » — which is the EVENT?
exercises.explanation: The imparfait (flânais) is the ongoing background; the passé composé (a appelé) is the event that cuts in.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Ne manquez pas le cœur historique de la ville.

options.text: Don't miss the historic heart of the city.
It's advisable to check before diving.
Avoid travelling during the rainy season.

exercises.prompt: What does « un carnet de voyage » mean?
exercises.explanation: un carnet de voyage = a travel diary.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « La brochure présente la région. » — a brochure.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “Let the souks tempt you.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Ne manquez pas le cœur historique de la ville.
exercises.targetEn: Don't miss the historic heart of the city.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Évitez de voyager pendant la saison des pluies.
exercises.targetEn: Avoid travelling during the rainy season.
